quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Reaction Types and Completing Equations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Identifying Reaction Types (Synthesis, Decomposition, Single Displacement, Double Displacement, Combustion, Neutralization, Redox), Completing Chemical Equations

questions.question:
$\text{MgCl}_2 + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{AgCl} + \text{Mg(NO}_3)_2$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is a double displacement reaction where the cations and anions exchange partners. There is no change in oxidation numbers (Mg remains +2, Cl -1, Ag +1, NO₃ -1), so it is not redox.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{Na} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{NaOH} + \text{H}_2$, the coefficient of $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{Na} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{NaOH} + \text{H}_2$. To balance, start with sodium: left has 1 Na, right has 1 Na in NaOH. Hydrogen: left has 2 H in H₂O, right has 1 H in NaOH + 2 H in H₂ = 3 H (unbalanced). Oxygen: left has 1 O, right has 1 O in NaOH (balanced). To balance hydrogen, use 2 H₂O (4 H on left), which requires 2 NaOH (2 H on right) plus H₂ (2 H) = 4 H on right. This also requires 2 Na on left. Therefore, the coefficient of H₂O is 2.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{C}_3\text{H}_8 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of $\text{CO}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $\text{C}_3\text{H}_8 + 5\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 3\text{CO}_2 + 4\text{H}_2\text{O}$. To balance, start with C₃H₈: 3 carbon atoms, so need 3 CO₂. Hydrogen: 8 H atoms, so need 4 H₂O. Oxygen: right side has (3 × 2) + (4 × 1) = 6 + 4 = 10 O atoms, so need 5 O₂ molecules. Therefore, the coefficient of CO₂ is 3.

questions.question:
$\text{Pb(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{KI} \rightarrow \text{PbI}_2 + 2\text{KNO}_3$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is a double displacement reaction forming lead(II) iodide (yellow precipitate) and potassium nitrate. No oxidation numbers change.

questions.question:
$\text{Zn} + \text{Cu(NO}_3)_2 \rightarrow \text{Zn(NO}_3)_2 + \text{Cu}$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is a single displacement (redox) reaction. Zinc displaces copper from copper nitrate. Zn is oxidized (0 → +2) and Cu is reduced (+2 → 0).

questions.question:
$\text{Ca} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{Ca(OH)}_2 + \text{H}_2$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is a single displacement (redox) reaction. Calcium displaces hydrogen from water. Ca is oxidized (0 → +2) and H is reduced (+1 → 0).

questions.question:
$2\text{NaHCO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 + \text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{CO}_2$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is a decomposition reaction where a single compound (sodium bicarbonate) breaks down into multiple products (sodium carbonate, water, and carbon dioxide).

questions.question:
$\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is an acid-base neutralization reaction. Sulfuric acid reacts with sodium hydroxide to produce a salt and water. No oxidation numbers change.

questions.question:
$\text{HNO}_3 + \text{KOH} \rightarrow \text{KNO}_3 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is an acid-base neutralization reaction. Nitric acid reacts with potassium hydroxide to form potassium nitrate and water.

questions.question:
$\text{HF} + \text{KOH} \rightarrow \text{KF} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is an acid-base neutralization reaction. Hydrofluoric acid reacts with potassium hydroxide to form potassium fluoride and water.

questions.question:
$\text{Zn} + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Zn(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{Ag}$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is both a redox and single displacement reaction. Zn is oxidized (0 → +2) and Ag is reduced (+1 → 0). Since it involves electron transfer, it is correctly classified as redox.

questions.question: $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 2\text{Al} \rightarrow 2\text{Fe} +$ _________
questions.explanation: $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 2\text{Al} \rightarrow 2\text{Fe} + \text{Al}_2\text{O}_3$ is the thermite reaction. Aluminum reduces iron(III) oxide to iron, forming aluminum oxide.

questions.question: $\text{TiCl}_4 + 2\text{Mg} \rightarrow \text{Ti} +$ __________
questions.explanation: $\text{TiCl}_4 + 2\text{Mg} \rightarrow \text{Ti} + 2\text{MgCl}_2$ is balanced. Magnesium reduces titanium(IV) chloride to titanium metal in the Kroll process.

questions.question: $\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2 + 3\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \rightarrow 3\text{CaSO}_4 +$ _________
questions.explanation: $\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2 + 3\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \rightarrow 3\text{CaSO}_4 + 2\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4$ is balanced. This is the production of phosphoric acid from calcium phosphate.

questions.question:
$2\text{NaCl} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{NaOH} + \text{H}_2 + \text{Cl}_2$

___ $\text{NaCl}$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ → ___ $\text{NaOH}$ + ___ $\text{H}_2$ + ___ $\text{Cl}_2$
questions.explanation: $2\text{NaCl} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{NaOH} + \text{H}_2 + \text{Cl}_2$ is balanced. This is the electrolysis of brine, producing sodium hydroxide, hydrogen gas, and chlorine gas.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered identifying reaction types and completing equations! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between single and double displacement reactions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on recognizing neutralization and redox reactions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the five main reaction types and how to complete balanced equations.
